2605.23384.title: Metacognition as Reward: Reinforcing LLM Reasoning via Knowledge and Regulation Signals
2605.23384.paper_summary: Recent RL methods have substantially improved the reasoning abilities of LLMs. Existing reward designs mainly follow two paradigms: (1) Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) derives outcome signals from executable checks or ground-truth answers, but provides limited guidance for intermediate reasoning behaviors. (2) Rubrics-as-reward (RaR) goes beyond final-answer checking by using natural-language rubrics to assess reasoning quality and task compliance, but often requires instance-specific rubrics and substantial design effort. To address these issues, we introduce Metacognition-as-Reward (MaR), a metacognition-inspired RL framework that guides LLM reasoning through two general process dimensions: i) metacognitive knowledge, which identifies task-relevant information without hand-crafted instance-specific rubrics, and ii) metacognitive regulation, which plans and adjusts the reasoning process to provide reward guidance beyond final-answer outcomes. MaR scaffolds model rollouts into explicit metacognitive components and optimizes them with a trajectory-level reward over task knowledge coverage, regulation fidelity, and final-answer correctness. In this way, MaR extends reward feedback to reasoning trajectories while grounding the reward signals in general metacognitive dimensions. Experiments on 22 benchmarks show that MaR consistently improves model performance, achieving up to a 7.7% gain over the base model and up to an 11.0% gain over vanilla DAPO. Notably, Qwen3.5-9B + MaR narrows the gap to frontier models, surpassing GPT-OSS-120B on overall average and outperforming stronger models on several individual benchmarks. Process-level analysis further shows substantial improvements in reasoning process quality. MaR also generalizes to out-of-domain datasets, where MaR-trained models improve over their corresponding base models on average.
2605.23384.paper_summary_zh: 最近的強化學習（RL）方法顯著提升了大型語言模型（LLMs）的推理能力。現有的獎勵設計主要遵循兩種範式：(1) 可驗證獎勵的強化學習（RLVR）從可執行的檢查或真實答案中推導結果信號，但對於中間推理行為提供的指導有限。(2) 作為獎勵的評分標準（RaR）通過使用自然語言評分標準來評估推理質量和任務合規性，超越了最終答案檢查，但通常需要特定實例的評分標準和大量的設計工作。為了解決這些問題，我們引入了以元認知為基礎的獎勵（MaR），這是一個受到元認知啟發的強化學習框架，通過兩個一般過程維度指導LLM推理：i) 元認知知識，識別與任務相關的信息，而無需手工製作的特定實例評分標準，ii) 元認知調節，計劃和調整推理過程，以提供超越最終答案結果的獎勵指導。MaR將模型的展開結構化為明確的元認知組件，並通過任務知識覆蓋、調節忠實度和最終答案正確性等軌跡級獎勵來優化它們。這樣，MaR將獎勵反饋擴展到推理軌跡，同時將獎勵信號基於一般的元認知維度。對22個基準的實驗顯示，MaR持續改善模型性能，較基礎模型提高了最高7.7%的增益，較普通的DAPO提高了最高11.0%的增益。值得注意的是，Qwen3.5-9B + MaR縮小了與前沿模型的差距，在整體平均上超越了GPT-OSS-120B，並在幾個單獨基準上超越了更強的模型。過程級分析進一步顯示推理過程質量的顯著改善。MaR也能夠推廣到域外數據集，在這些數據集中，經過MaR訓練的模型在平均上優於其相應的基礎模型。
2605.23384.author: Sirui Chen et.al.
2605.23384.authors: Sirui Chen, Lei Xu, Yuying Zhao, Yutian Chen, Yu Wang, Beier Zhu, Hanwang Zhang, Shengjie Zhao, Chaochao Lu
